name: Baked Flounder with Scallop Stuffing
recipeInstructions:
1. In a 10-inch skillet, melt the 1/2 cup of butter over medium heat.
2. Add garlic and onion; sauté until onions are translucent (about 3-4 minutes).
3. Add scallops and cook for an additional 2-3 minutes, stirring occasionally.
4. Season with salt, pepper, and white wine to taste. Mix in enough bread crumbs to form a moist stuffing.
5. Place each flounder fillet, dark-side-up, on a flat surface or tray.
6. Spoon the scallop stuffing into the center of each fillet; divide evenly among the fillets.
7. Fold both ends of each fillet over the stuffing, overlapping slightly.
8. In a 9x12 inch baking dish, pour melted butter and hot water.
9. Transfer the stuffed fillets to the baking dish, arranging them in one layer.
10. Bake for 20 minutes while preparing the white sauce.
11. White Sauce: In a small saucepan, melt butter over low heat. Whisk in flour, cooking 2-3 minutes until smooth.
12. Gradually add milk and season with salt, pepper, and white wine to taste. Increase heat to medium and whisk constantly until thickened (about 5-7 minutes).
13. When the flounder is done baking after 20 minutes, pour the sauce over the stuffed fillets.
14. Return the dish to the oven briefly to heat through and allow the sauce to bubble.
(Note: This recipe does not include attribution or additional notes.)
